content: Define the TopModule with its inputs and outputs. Include clk, reset, and in_valid as inputs, and out as an output. This setup will serve as the foundation for further implementation.
source: Implement the same functionality and interface as `original.txt`.

content: Implement the compute_core module with necessary inputs and outputs derived from TopModule. This module will handle the main computation logic, taking inputs from TopModule and providing the computed output back to TopModule.
source: Keep behavior identical and keep required module split (`TopModule`, `compute_core`, `control_fsm`).

content: Implement the control_fsm module which will manage the control signals based on the input in_valid and the internal state of the FSM. This module will output control signals to compute_core to manage the computation process.
source: Keep behavior identical and keep required module split (`TopModule`, `compute_core`, `control_fsm`).

content: In TopModule, implement the logic to handle the reset functionality. This includes setting the output to a default state when reset is active.
source: The reset input is active high synchronous and should reset the output to zero.

content: In control_fsm, define the FSM states and transitions based on the in_valid signal. This will determine how the FSM moves between states and controls the compute_core.
source: The module should implement a finite-state machine that takes as input a serial bit stream and outputs a one whenever the bit stream contains two consecutive one's.

content: In compute_core, implement the logic to perform the computation only when enabled by the control_fsm. This includes gating the computation logic with the control signals to reduce power consumption.
source: Optimization direction: power-first. - reduce unnecessary switching when `in_valid=0`

content: In TopModule, implement the structural connections that link the compute_core and control_fsm modules to the TopModule's inputs and outputs. This includes wiring the control signals and data paths appropriately.
source: Keep behavior identical and keep required module split (`TopModule`, `compute_core`, `control_fsm`).
